sections.title: Overview
sections.content: A metered dose inhaler (MDI) with a spacer chamber delivers inhaled medication to the lower airways more effectively than an MDI alone. Using a spacer eliminates the need for precise hand-breath coordination, increases lung deposition, and reduces oral steroid deposition. Used for bronchodilators (salbutamol, ipratropium) in asthma and COPD.

sections.title: Procedure
sections.content:
**With spacer (preferred):**

1. Shake MDI vigorously for 5 seconds

2. Remove cap from MDI and spacer mouthpiece

3. Insert MDI into back of spacer

4. Ask patient to exhale fully

5. Seal lips around spacer mouthpiece (or apply mask for children/compromised patients)

6. Press MDI once to release one actuation

7. Patient takes 4–5 slow deep breaths through the spacer (5 second breath-hold if possible)

8. Wait 30–60 seconds between actuations

9. Repeat for prescribed number of doses

**Mask attachment (infants/altered consciousness):**
- Attach appropriately sized mask to spacer in place of mouthpiece
- Apply snugly to face; actuate and allow 5–6 tidal breaths per actuation

sections.title: Key Points
sections.content:
- One actuation at a time — do not stack multiple puffs then inhale
- Document: drug, number of actuations, patient response (SpO₂, auscultation, work of breathing)
- Spacer should be cleaned periodically — static charge reduces drug delivery
- For severe asthma: nebuliser is preferred over MDI/spacer when available
